extracted_codes.context: See file: PNAabxcuis.csv on phekb for CUI codes matching list.

clinical_criteria: Age >= 18 at time of radiology pneumonia event
At least 1 non-negated radiology report mentioning 'pneumonia'
At least 2 mentions of ICD9/10 pneumonia codes from Appendix A within 31 days prior and 31 days following radiology event
At least 1 mention of antibiotic treatment from Appendix B within 31 days prior and 31 days following radiology event
Medical home definition: 3 or more primary care visits in 2 years (for controls)
Exclusion if >= 2 occurrences of Group A exclusion codes on different dates within one year before or after radiology PNA event
Exclusion if >= 2 occurrences of Group B exclusion codes on different dates anytime before or up to one year after radiology PNA event
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm identifies bacterial pneumonia cases by combining three main criteria: (1) non-negated radiology reports mentioning 'pneumonia' using natural language processing, (2) at least 2 ICD9/10 pneumonia diagnosis codes within a 62-day window around the radiology event, and (3) at least 1 antibiotic prescription within the same window. Cases are excluded if they have multiple occurrences of exclusion conditions like pregnancy, addiction, immunodeficiency, asthma, COPD, malignancy, etc. Controls are defined as subjects with adequate medical care (3+ primary care visits in 2 years) who either have no pneumonia codes or only negated pneumonia mentions in radiology reports, and who don't meet exclusion criteria. The algorithm creates a 6-month window (1 month prior, 5 months after) around the index pneumonia event for analysis.
